subject: Mathematics
title: Golden Ratio Myths Debunked
description: Learn Golden Ratio Myths Debunked in one minute.

hook.text: The internet claims the golden ratio makes faces beautiful and seashells perfect. Most of it is wrong.

buildup.text: Nautilus shells follow a logarithmic spiral, but its ratio is about 1.33—not 1.618.

discovery.text: Studies on facial beauty find no consistent link to phi. Symmetry matters far more than ratios.

twist.text: The claim that credit cards are golden rectangles is also false. Their ratio is about 1.586.

climax.text: Phi is genuinely fascinating without the myths. Its real appearances in math and nature are remarkable enough.

punchline.text: The golden ratio is real magic dressed in fake myths.

quiz.question: What is the actual spiral ratio of a nautilus shell?
